zotero.abstractNote: Astronomical images often suffer from glare, a phenomenon where light from bright sources is scattered by the optical system, obscuring faint details in their vicinity. This is particularly problematic in high-dynamic-range (HDR) scenes, where the intensity difference between the bright source and the faint details can span several orders of magnitude. We propose a novel method for glare removal that leverages a deep learning approach to model the point spread function (PSF) of the optical system. Our method is trained on a large dataset of synthetic astronomical images with realistic glare artifacts. We demonstrate that our approach effectively removes glare while preserving the structural integrity of faint astronomical objects, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods on both synthetic and real-world astronomical data.
